revision.text: <noinclude>{{Hatnote|This template provides a see-also section for various templates.}}</noinclude>{{navbar|List of nowrap-like templates|plain=y|style=float:right}} * {{tl|nowrap}} prevents wrapping within a single template (i.e. not suitable for relatively complex or lengthy content). * {{tl|nowraplinks}} ... {{tl|nowraplinks end}} prevents wraps inside links while allowing wraps between links and in normal text. Very useful for link lists and easy to use. * {{tl|nowrap begin}} ... {{tl|nowrap end}} prevents wraps in both text and links. For the trickier wrapping cases when you need full control, for instance in very complex link lists. * {{tl|word joiner}} prevents wraps for both two adjacent characters. * {{tl|avoid wrap}} prevents wraps unless the space is too narrow to display the text on one line. ** {{tl|inline block}} does the same and allows further style customization, but does not automatically add the "avoidwrap" CSS class. * {{tl|spaces}} produces multiple non-breaking spaces (or a single one). * {{tl|wrap}} can be used to provide a (brief) exception within a no-wrapping area. ** {{tl|normalwraplink}} allows links to wrap when they otherwise would not. * {{tn|·}} and {{tn|•}}, spaced and wrap-sensitive [[interpunct]] (dot) and bullet. * [[Help:Line-break handling]], Wikipedia's how-to guide about word wrapping and line breaks. ** In particular, to avoid line wrapping when quoting a passage such as a poem or computer code, see {{slink|Template:Quote|Line breaks}}{{snd}}e.g. Template:Blockquote with "poem" tag.<noinclude> [[Category:Documentation see also templates]] </noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: <noinclude>{{Hatnote|This template provides a see-also section for various templates.}}</noinclude>{{navbar|List of nowrap-like templates|plain=y|style=float:right}} * {{tl|nowrap}} prevents wrapping within a single template (i.e. not suitable for relatively complex or lengthy content). * {{tl|nowraplinks}} ... {{tl|nowraplinks end}} prevents wraps inside links while allowing wraps between links and in normal text. Very useful for link lists and easy to use. * {{tl|nowrap begin}} ... {{tl|nowrap end}} prevents wraps in both text and links. For the trickier wrapping cases when you need full control, for instance in very complex link lists. * {{tl|word joiner}} prevents wraps for both two adjacent characters. * {{tl|avoid wrap}} prevents wraps unless the space is too narrow to display the text on one line. ** {{tl|inline block}} does the same and allows further style customization, but does not automatically add the "avoidwrap" CSS class. * {{tl|spaces}} produces multiple non-breaking spaces (or a single one). * {{tl|wrap}} can be used to provide a (brief) exception within a no-wrapping area. ** {{tl|normalwraplink}} allows links to wrap when they otherwise would not. * {{tn|·}} and {{tn|•}}, spaced and wrap-sensitive [[:en:Interpunct|Interpunct]] (dot) and bullet. * [[Help:Line-break handling]], Wikipedia's how-to guide about word wrapping and line breaks. ** In particular, to avoid line wrapping when quoting a passage such as a poem or computer code, see {{slink|Template:Quote|Line breaks}}{{snd}}e.g. Template:Blockquote with "poem" tag.<noinclude> [[Category:Documentation see also templates]] </noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: <noinclude>{{Hatnote|This template provides a see-also section for various templates.}}</noinclude>{{navbar|List of nowrap-like templates|plain=y|style=float:right}} * {{tl|nowrap}} prevents wrapping within a single template (i.e. not suitable for relatively complex or lengthy content). * {{tl|nowraplinks}} ... {{tl|nowraplinks end}} prevents wraps inside links while allowing wraps between links and in normal text. Very useful for link lists and easy to use. * {{tl|nowrap begin}} ... {{tl|nowrap end}} prevents wraps in both text and links. For the trickier wrapping cases when you need full control, for instance in very complex link lists. * {{tl|word joiner}} prevents wraps for both two adjacent characters. * {{tl|avoid wrap}} prevents wraps unless the space is too narrow to display the text on one line. ** {{tl|inline block}} does the same and allows further style customization, but does not automatically add the "avoidwrap" CSS class. * {{tl|spaces}} produces multiple non-breaking spaces (or a single one). * {{tl|wrap}} can be used to provide a (brief) exception within a no-wrapping area. ** {{tl|normalwraplink}} allows links to wrap when they otherwise would not. * {{tn|·}} and {{tn|•}}, spaced and wrap-sensitive [[:en:Interpunct|Interpunct]] (dot) and bullet. * [[Help:Line-break handling]], Wikipedia's how-to guide about word wrapping and line breaks. ** In particular, to avoid line wrapping when quoting a passage such as a poem or computer code, see {{slink|Template:Quote|Line breaks}}{{snd}}e.g. Template:Blockquote with "poem" tag.<noinclude> [[Category:Documentation see also templates]] </noinclude> <noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: <noinclude>{{Hatnote|This template provides a see-also section for various templates.}}</noinclude>{{navbar|List of nowrap-like templates|plain=y|style=float:right}} * {{tl|nowrap}} prevents wrapping within a single template (i.e. not suitable for relatively complex or lengthy content). * {{tl|nowraplinks}} ... {{tl|nowraplinks end}} prevents wraps inside links while allowing wraps between links and in normal text. Very useful for link lists and easy to use. * {{tl|nowrap begin}} ... {{tl|nowrap end}} prevents wraps in both text and links. For the trickier wrapping cases when you need full control, for instance in very complex link lists. * {{tl|word joiner}} prevents wraps for both two adjacent characters. * {{tl|avoid wrap}} prevents wraps unless the space is too narrow to display the text on one line. ** {{tl|inline block}} does the same and allows further style customization, but does not automatically add the "avoidwrap" CSS class. * {{tl|spaces}} produces multiple non-breaking spaces (or a single one). * {{tl|wrap}} can be used to provide a (brief) exception within a no-wrapping area. ** {{tl|normalwraplink}} allows links to wrap when they otherwise would not. * {{tn|·}} and {{tn|•}}, spaced and wrap-sensitive [[:en:Interpunct|Interpunct]] (dot) and bullet. * [[Help:Line-break handling]], Wikipedia's how-to guide about word wrapping and line breaks. ** In particular, to avoid line wrapping when quoting a passage such as a poem or computer code, see {{slink|Template:Quote|Line breaks}}{{snd}}e.g. Template:Blockquote with "poem" tag.<noinclude> [[Category:Documentation see also templates]] </noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: <noinclude>{{Hatnote|This template provides a see-also section for various templates.}}</noinclude>{{navbar|List of nowrap-like templates|plain=y|style=float:right}} * {{tl|nowrap}} prevents wrapping within a single template (i.e. not suitable for relatively complex or lengthy content). * {{tl|nowraplinks}} ... {{tl|nowraplinks end}} prevents wraps inside links while allowing wraps between links and in normal text. Very useful for link lists and easy to use. * {{tl|nowrap begin}} ... {{tl|nowrap end}} prevents wraps in both text and links. For the trickier wrapping cases when you need full control, for instance in very complex link lists. * {{tl|word joiner}} prevents wraps for both two adjacent characters. * {{tl|avoid wrap}} prevents wraps unless the space is too narrow to display the text on one line. ** {{tl|inline block}} does the same and allows further style customization, but does not automatically add the "avoidwrap" CSS class. * {{tl|spaces}} produces multiple non-breaking spaces (or a single one). * {{tl|wrap}} can be used to provide a (brief) exception within a no-wrapping area. ** {{tl|normalwraplink}} allows links to wrap when they otherwise would not. * {{tn|·}} and {{tn|•}}, spaced and wrap-sensitive [[:en:Interpunct|Interpunct]] (dot) and bullet. * [[Help:Line-break handling]], Wikipedia's how-to guide about word wrapping and line breaks. ** In particular, to avoid line wrapping when quoting a passage such as a poem or computer code, see {{slink|Template:Quote|Line breaks}}{{snd}}e.g. Template:Blockquote with "poem" tag.<noinclude> [[Category:Documentation see also templates]] </noinclude> [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 1 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23511227343|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: <noinclude>{{Hatnote|This template provides a see-also section for various templates.}}</noinclude>{{navbar|List of nowrap-like templates|plain=y|style=float:right}} * {{tl|nowrap}} prevents wrapping within a single template (i.e. not suitable for relatively complex or lengthy content). * {{tl|nowraplinks}} ... {{tl|nowraplinks end}} prevents wraps inside links while allowing wraps between links and in normal text. Very useful for link lists and easy to use. * {{tl|nowrap begin}} ... {{tl|nowrap end}} prevents wraps in both text and links. For the trickier wrapping cases when you need full control, for instance in very complex link lists. * {{tl|word joiner}} prevents wraps for both two adjacent characters. * {{tl|avoid wrap}} prevents wraps unless the space is too narrow to display the text on one line. ** {{tl|inline block}} does the same and allows further style customization, but does not automatically add the "avoidwrap" CSS class. * {{tl|spaces}} produces multiple non-breaking spaces (or a single one). * {{tl|wrap}} can be used to provide a (brief) exception within a no-wrapping area. ** {{tl|normalwraplink}} allows links to wrap when they otherwise would not. * {{tn|·}} and {{tn|•}}, spaced and wrap-sensitive [[:en:Interpunct|Interpunct]] (dot) and bullet. * [[Help:Line-break handling]], Wikipedia's how-to guide about word wrapping and line breaks. ** In particular, to avoid line wrapping when quoting a passage such as a poem or computer code, see {{slink|Template:Quote|Line breaks}}{{snd}}e.g. Template:Blockquote with "poem" tag.<noinclude> [[Category:Documentation see also templates]] </noinclude> <noinclude> [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>

<noinclude>{{Hatnote|This template provides a see-also section for various templates.}}</noinclude>{{navbar|List of nowrap-like templates|plain=y|style=float:right}} * {{tl|nowrap}} prevents wrapping within a single template (i.e. not suitable for relatively complex or lengthy content). * {{tl|nowraplinks}} ... {{tl|nowraplinks end}} prevents wraps inside links while allowing wraps between links and in normal text. Very useful for link lists and easy to use. * {{tl|nowrap begin}} ... {{tl|nowrap end}} prevents wraps in both text and links. For the trickier wrapping cases when you need full control, for instance in very complex link lists. * {{tl|word joiner}} prevents wraps for both two adjacent characters. * {{tl|avoid wrap}} prevents wraps unless the space is too narrow to display the text on one line. ** {{tl|inline block}} does the same and allows further style customization, but does not automatically add the "avoidwrap" CSS class. * {{tl|spaces}} produces multiple non-breaking spaces (or a single one). * {{tl|wrap}} can be used to provide a (brief) exception within a no-wrapping area. ** {{tl|normalwraplink}} allows links to wrap when they otherwise would not. * {{tn|·}} and {{tn|•}}, spaced and wrap-sensitive [[:en:Interpunct|Interpunct]] (dot) and bullet. * [[Help:Line-break handling]], Wikipedia's how-to guide about word wrapping and line breaks. ** In particular, to avoid line wrapping when quoting a passage such as a poem or computer code, see {{slink|Template:Quote|Line breaks}}{{snd}}e.g. Template:Blockquote with "poem" tag.<noinclude> [[Category:Documentation see also templates]]
[[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>
